brief_summary: Horizontal bar chart on a 0-10 g/cm3 axis comparing density of copper (~8.9), brass (~8.5, bronze-highlighted as the mod's default material), steel (~7.85), and aluminum (~2.7, labelled as the case material around the weight). Demonstrates why brass/steel/copper are the materials of choice for a weight mod rather than more aluminum.
data_sources: brass-weight-mod.mdx § 'The sound change is real and modest' — 'brass runs roughly 8.4–8.7 g/cm³, copper close to 8.9 g/cm³, and steel around 7.85 g/cm³ — all three to four times denser than the 2.7 g/cm³ of the aluminum case around them'
